item.title: All NTE: Neverness to Everness Pre-Registration Rewards & How to Get Them
item.content:encoded:
A slew of big titles are releasing in the next month, which could stretch your bank accounts, but Neverness to Everness offers some hope with a free-to-play model, and a host of pre-registration rewards are available.

Neverness to Everness launches on April 29 and has attracted vast attention pre-launch, with millions of players across the world eagerly awaiting the launch of the Gacha title.

A huge chunk have already qualified for pre-registration rewards, and with Insider Gaming’s help, you can join them.

All Neverness to Everness Pre-Registration Rewards

There are five pre-registration rewards available in Neverness to Everness, alongside an additional reward for a social media milestone.

Gamers around the world have united to unlock the full slew of available pre-registration rewards, with over 35 million users resulting in an influx of bonuses for those who registered.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is NTE: Neverness to Everness on PlayStation, Xbox & PC? All Platforms, Explained

Additionally, the official account followers of Neverness to Everness on social media have unlocked an additional reward, providing players with even more freebies.

The table below shows all of the Neverness to Everness Pre-Registration rewards, along with the community landmarks and other requirements for obtaining.

How to Get Neverness to Everness Pre-Registration Rewards

To earn pre-registration rewards in Neverness to Everness, players must pre-register for the title on the official website.

The process doesn’t take long, and once completed, the available rewards will be distributed via the in-game mailbox at launch.

  • More Insider Gaming: Neverness to Everness Pre-Download Guide—How to Preload & File Sizes Explained

Here’s the step-by-step process to pre-registration.

  1. Head to the official Neverness to Everness website.
  2. Hit the ‘Preregister Now’ button at the top of the page.
  3. Complete the steps outlined.
  4. After launch, claim the rewards through the in-game Mailbox.

You may have to complete early parts of the game to unlock features like the Mailbox, but it shouldn’t take too much time, based on similar titles.

Are you excited for Neverness to Everness? Share your hopes for the game in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Neverness to Everness, find out about the game’s multiplayer features, and check the exact start time for launch.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia Sableye’s Gem Hunt Event: Release Date, Start Times & All Content
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Pokopia continues to bring new content post-launch, and a new event is on the horizon, introducing Sableye to the game, along with fresh items.

Pokopia’s widely successful launch has opened the door for continued content drops, with fans hoping that full DLC will be available later down the line, and it’s clear there are plans for fresh content, with Sableye set to become the fourth post-launch addition to the roster.

If you’re excited for the Pokémon Pokopia Sableye’s Gem Hunt event and want all the details on when it kicks off, Insider Gaming has you covered.

When is The Pokémon Pokopia Sableye’s Gem Hunt Event?

The Sableye’s Gem Hunt Event in Pokémon Pokopia kicks off on April 29, 2026, running through to a conclusion on May 14.

This marks the third post-launch event for Pokopia, following on from an event that introduced Hoppip, Skiploom, and Jumpluff to the game, and an April Fool’s event with an Inflatable Sudowoodo reward.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Pokémon in Pokopia & How to Get Them

Like the previous events, the content is actually available now if you time skip on your console by setting your time and date to be within these periods, but there’s no real benefit to unlocking the content early.

Given that the event is also due to run for an extensive period of time, you won’t need to rush through it, so the best approach is to just wait until launch.

What Time Does the Pokémon Pokopia Sableye’s Gem Hunt Event Start?

The Pokémon Pokopia Sableye’s Gem Hunt event begins at 5 AM local time on April 29, running for over two weeks.

As the event has a set structure with a staggered start time globally, you won’t need to wait for an awkward time in your location. Instead, it will be available on April 29 as long as you log in after 5 AM.

In total, the event runs for over two weeks, ending at 04:59 AM local time on May 14. After this date has passed, content from the event will be unavailable.

However, you may be able to re-access the event content by changing your console time and date back to the event period, though this won’t be confirmed until after the event concludes.

All Content in the Pokémon Pokopia Sableye’s Gem Hunt Event

The main highlight of the Sableye’s Gem Hunt Event in Pokémon Pokopia is the introduction of Sableye to the roster, adding a fourth event-exclusive Pokémon to the game.

Sableye will come alongside a new habitat and several new items, including camping-themed decorations and buildings, like a Tent Kit, Sleeping Bag, Camping Chair, and Digital Camera.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Pokopia Pokémon Habitats & How to Make Them

Similar to the Hoppip event, new items will be obtained by exchanging event-specific currency, in this case, Red Crystal Fragments. Items will be purchaseable through the shop inside any rebuilt Pokémon Center.

If you miss out on any rewards or want extra, even after the event has passed, you can use the camera function and the 3D printer to replicate the item.

What other Pokémon do you want to see added through events in Pokopia? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon Pokopia, find out what happened to the humans in the game, and learn what to do with Rainbow Feathers.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 24, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Creamy-white material
  • Hint 2: Found in tusks
  • Hint 3: _____ Coast

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Turquoise
  • Bronze
  • Rug
  • Satin
  • Mauve
  • Burgundy
  • Silk
  • Taupe
  • Beige

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 24, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Ivory

Ivory is the hard, creamy-white modified dentine that composes the tusks of a tusked animal, like an elephant or a walrus.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on April 25.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • NYT Connections
  • Wordle

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1048)—Answers for Friday, April 24
item.content:encoded:
That’s the end of another week! I hope you’ve had a brilliant few days and that your NYT Connections streak is going strong. Today, we can take that one step further and produce yet another win. Below, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to get today’s puzzle ticked off as quickly as possible.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 24)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • TOMATO SAUCE
  • HELICOPTER
  • TAXI
  • INTELLIGENCE
  • ICE SKATES
  • RED WINE
  • GRASS
  • LAWN MOWER
  • INK
  • CABIN
  • DOUGH
  • SUCTION CUPS
  • CHEESE
  • CALLOWAY
  • ARMS
  • PEPPERONI

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things you’d associate with pizza
  • Green – Things you’d associate with an octopus or squid
  • Blue – Things that have blades
  • Purple – Things you might nickname ‘cab‘

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 24:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 24 (#1048)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – CHEESE, DOUGH, PEPPERONI, TOMATO SAUCE
  • Green – ARMS, INK, INTELLIGENCE, SUCTION CUPS
  • Blue – GRASS, HELICOPTER, ICE SKATES, LAWN MOWER
  • Purple – CABIN, CALLOWAY, RED WINE, TAXI

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
